evals.prompt: Write a README for my project. It's a Node.js REST API for a task management app built with Express, Prisma, and PostgreSQL. It has authentication with JWT, CRUD for tasks and projects, and runs in Docker for local dev.
evals.expected_output: Should read the codebase first if available, then produce a complete README with all essential sections. Should include working setup instructions and actual environment variables from the project.
assertions: Reads codebase/package.json before writing if available
Starts with a clear one-line description of what the project does
Includes prerequisites with specific versions
Setup instructions go from git clone to running server
Includes Docker setup instructions
Lists environment variables needed with descriptions
Includes verification step (e.g., 'visit localhost:3000/health')
Code examples are copy-pasteable
Saves the README.md file to the project root

evals.prompt: I need to document the API endpoints of my Express app. We have about 15 endpoints across users, tasks, and projects resources.
evals.expected_output: Should read the route files, then produce organized API documentation grouped by resource with request/response examples.
assertions: Reads the actual route/controller files to discover endpoints
Groups endpoints by resource (users, tasks, projects)
Documents method, path, description for each endpoint
Includes request body schema for POST/PUT endpoints
Includes response shape examples
Documents error responses (400, 401, 404)
Notes authentication requirements per endpoint

evals.prompt: Generate a changelog from our recent git history. We're about to release v2.1.0.
evals.expected_output: Should read git log, categorize commits into Added/Changed/Fixed/etc., and produce a Keep a Changelog formatted entry.
assertions: Reads git log to find changes since last tag/release
Categorizes changes into Keep a Changelog sections
Writes entries from user perspective, not developer perspective
Includes the version number and date
Includes comparison link to previous version
Does not include trivial commits (merge commits, typo fixes) unless relevant
